meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Solve Debugging Issues with AI Workflows
content.seo_description: Struggling to debug client code? Learn how to optimize your freelance workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: Stop manually chasing bugs. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for automated CLI-based debugging, you transform your workflow from reactive fixing to proactive engineering. This combination allows you to isolate logic errors, generate unit tests, and resolve complex code issues in minutes, not hours.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your terminal is bleeding red error messages. You’re staring at a codebase that feels like a labyrinth, and the pressure to ship is mounting. It’s the classic freelance trap: you’re paid to build, but you’re stuck playing digital detective, chasing ghosts in the machine while your hourly rate effectively plummets.

But what if you could outsource the grunt work of debugging to a system that never sleeps? By shifting your process from manual troubleshooting to an AI-augmented workflow, you stop being a code-fixer and start being an architect. We’re moving away from the 'trial and error' slog and into a high-velocity, automated paradigm.

steps.title: Establish the Contextual Baseline
steps.description: Before debugging, you must provide the context of the current issue to the AI to avoid hallucinated fixes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided codebase and map the data flow between the authentication module and the database service. Identify where the state mismatch occurs.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Points
steps.description: Use automated analysis to pinpoint the exact line of code causing the runtime crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a dependency impact analysis on the current crash log. List the 3 most likely functions causing this failure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'grep -r "Error:" ./logs' --ask 'Find the source of this error in the codebase'

steps.title: Generate Defensive Unit Tests
steps.description: Create tests that capture the bug so you can verify the fix immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test suite that reproduces the current null-pointer exception in the user profile component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write ./tests/profile.test.ts --prompt 'Generate unit tests to reproduce the null-pointer error'

steps.title: Execute Targeted Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the fix while maintaining architectural integrity across the project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for the identified faulty function that handles edge-case errors without changing the public API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/components/Profile.tsx --prompt 'Apply the refactor to handle null states safely'

steps.title: Verify and Validate
steps.description: Run the full test suite to ensure no regressions were introduced during the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the applied changes and confirm they align with the original architectural requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm test' --ask 'Verify all tests pass and explain the fix'

pro_tips: Always use the --ask flag in Claude Code to force the AI to explain its reasoning before it executes a file write.
Map your project structure in Antigravity once, then use that 'Graph Context' as a system prompt for all subsequent debugging sessions.
When debugging complex state, ask the AI to 'trace the lifecycle of the object' rather than just 'fix the error'.
Keep a 'debug-history.md' file in your repo; use Claude Code to auto-summarize your fixes so you have documentation for your clients.
Use Antigravity to identify 'code smells' before they become bugs; proactive refactoring reduces your support burden by 40%.
content.next_steps: Integrate these tools into your CI/CD pipeline so that every commit is automatically analyzed for potential regressions before it hits the production environment.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start scaling your freelance business. With this workflow, you'll spend less time debugging and more time shipping high-value features that keep your clients coming back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
